phases.name: Year 1
phases.subtitle: Explore
phases.description: Introduction to agricultural education and professional service career pathways in extension and USDA agencies.

milestones.label: Explore ag education & extension careers
milestones.description: Projected curriculum — subject to faculty verification. Survey of agricultural education with emphasis on non-teaching professional service careers in Cooperative Extension, USDA, and agricultural communications.

milestones.label: Develop professional writing skills
milestones.description: Academic and professional writing foundations for agricultural communications, grant writing, and extension publications.

milestones.label: Build agricultural science foundations
milestones.description: Biological science foundations supporting agricultural content knowledge for extension education and outreach.

milestones.label: Master public communication
milestones.description: Projected curriculum — subject to faculty verification. Public speaking and presentation skills for community outreach, stakeholder engagement, and agricultural communications.

phases.name: Year 2
phases.subtitle: Engage
phases.description: Agricultural communication, program development, and community engagement build professional service competencies.

milestones.label: Study agricultural communications
milestones.description: Projected curriculum — subject to faculty verification. Media production, digital communication, and public information strategies for agricultural agencies and extension services.

milestones.label: Master youth & community program design
milestones.description: 4-H program management, community needs assessment, and youth leadership development. Students design agricultural outreach programs for diverse community audiences.

milestones.label: Understand rural communities
milestones.description: Social structures, rural sociology, and community dynamics that inform extension programming and agricultural policy development.

milestones.label: Build agribusiness knowledge
milestones.description: Agribusiness fundamentals providing economic context for extension education and USDA service programs.

phases.name: Year 3
phases.subtitle: Develop
phases.description: Advanced extension methods, adult education, and grant writing develop professional service expertise.

milestones.label: Master adult education methods
milestones.description: Adult learning theory, facilitation skills, and program delivery methods for Cooperative Extension and USDA outreach programs.

milestones.label: Study extension program planning
milestones.description: Projected curriculum — subject to faculty verification. Cooperative Extension program planning, needs assessment, logic model development, and program evaluation methods.

milestones.label: Develop grant writing skills
milestones.description: Projected curriculum — subject to faculty verification. Federal and state grant writing for agricultural programs including USDA-NIFA, Extension, and community development funding.

milestones.label: Complete extension internship
milestones.description: Projected curriculum — subject to faculty verification. Supervised internship at a Cooperative Extension office, USDA service center, agricultural nonprofit, or government agency.

phases.name: Year 4
phases.subtitle: Launch
phases.description: Capstone project, leadership development, and professional placement finalize career readiness.

milestones.label: Complete professional service capstone
milestones.courseRef: AGED 480 — Capstone in Agricultural Professional Service
milestones.description: Projected curriculum — subject to faculty verification. Integrative capstone project addressing a real extension or agricultural service challenge. Students develop comprehensive program proposals evaluated by faculty and agency professionals.

milestones.label: Study agricultural leadership
milestones.description: Projected curriculum — subject to faculty verification. Leadership theory, organizational change, and stakeholder management in agricultural organizations and government agencies.

milestones.label: Explore global agricultural development
milestones.description: Projected curriculum — subject to faculty verification. Global food security, international agricultural development, and cross-cultural extension education. Connections to USAID and international agricultural organizations.

milestones.label: Finalize professional placement
milestones.description: Career preparation including USDA application processes, Cooperative Extension hiring timelines, and professional networking at agricultural conferences.
